title: The Courtyard's Crown
storySnippet: At the heart of the courtyard, Lira faces the final challenge and claims the Crown of Context.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The march of time is ___, and no force on Earth can slow it down.
data.definition: Inexorable: impossible to stop or prevent; continuing without any possibility of being changed.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ negotiator refused to change her position despite hours of discussion.
data.definition: Intransigent: unwilling or refusing to change one's views or to agree; stubbornly uncompromising.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ coach gave a simple nod instead of a long speech after the team's victory.
data.definition: Laconic: using very few words to express something; brief and to the point.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ witness told lie after lie on the stand, contradicting every known fact.
data.definition: Mendacious: not telling the truth; lying or habitually dishonest.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The spy tried to ___ the truth by mixing in false details with real ones.
data.definition: Obfuscate: to make something unclear, confusing, or difficult to understand.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The security guard gave the badges only a ___ glance before waving everyone through.
data.definition: Perfunctory: carried out with minimal effort or care; done as a routine duty without real interest.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: His ___ dream of building a castle out of recycled bottles inspired the whole school.
data.definition: Quixotic: exceedingly idealistic; unrealistic and impractical, yet admirably noble in intent.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ assistant constantly praised the director's ideas, even the bad ones.
data.definition: Sycophantic: behaving in an excessively flattering way to gain advantage; fawning.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: A wave of ___ swept over the actor as she stepped onto the stage for her first performance.
data.definition: Trepidation: a feeling of fear or anxiety about something that may happen.

exercises.prompt: Choose the word that best completes the sentence.

data.sentence: The ___ review of the film was so harsh that it discouraged people from seeing it.
data.definition: Vituperative: bitter and abusive in language; harshly critical.
